mission.title: Install and Configure MicroK8s
mission.description: MicroK8s is a lightweight, single-package Kubernetes distribution ideal for development, IoT, and edge computing. It simplifies Kubernetes installation and management with minimal overhead, making it perfect for local development environments. However, it may not be suitable for large-scale production deployments due to its single-node architecture.

steps.title: Install MicroK8s
steps.description:
This step installs MicroK8s using the Snap package manager, which is the recommended method for installation.

```bash

sudo snap install microk8s --classic

```

> Use --classic for full access to system resources.

steps.title: Add User to MicroK8s Group
steps.description:
This step allows your user to run MicroK8s commands without sudo by adding your user to the microk8s group.

```bash

sudo usermod -a -G microk8s $USER

```

> Replace $USER with your username if not using the current user.

steps.title: Verify MicroK8s Installation
steps.description:
This step checks if MicroK8s is running correctly and lists the nodes in the cluster.

```bash

sudo microk8s kubectl get nodes

```

> This command should return the current node status.

steps.title: Enable Add-ons
steps.description:
This step enables essential Kubernetes add-ons such as DNS and the dashboard for better management.

```bash

sudo microk8s enable dns

sudo microk8s enable dashboard

```

> Enables DNS for service discovery.
> Enables the Kubernetes dashboard for UI management.

uninstall.title: Uninstall MicroK8s
uninstall.description:
This step removes MicroK8s from your system. Be aware that this will delete all Kubernetes resources and configurations.

```bash

sudo snap remove microk8s

```

> This will remove MicroK8s and all associated data.

uninstall.title: Remove User from MicroK8s Group
uninstall.description:
This step removes your user from the microk8s group to prevent access to MicroK8s commands.

```bash

sudo gpasswd -d $USER microk8s

```

> Replace $USER with your username if not using the current user.

upgrade.title: Upgrade MicroK8s
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your configurations and resources. This step upgrades MicroK8s to the latest version.

```bash

sudo snap refresh microk8s

sudo microk8s kubectl get nodes

```

> This command will upgrade MicroK8s to the latest stable version.
> Verify that the nodes are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'microk8s is not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** MicroK8s service may not be started.

**Fix:**
Run 'sudo microk8s start' to start the MicroK8s service.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubectl command is not in your PATH.

**Fix:**
Use 'sudo microk8s kubectl' or add the microk8s binary to your PATH.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Kubeconfig may not be set up correctly.

**Fix:**
Run 'sudo microk8s kubectl config view --raw > $HOME/.kube/config' to set up kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The host machine may not meet the minimum resource requirements.

**Fix:**
Ensure your machine has at least 2 vCPU and 4GB RAM.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes v1.31 features, including improved scheduling and resource management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get nodes' showing the status of your node as 'Ready'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure your system meets the minimum requirements and has Snap installed.
